title: Register AzureFoundryProvider in goose provider registry
description:
Wire AzureFoundryProvider into the provider selection/factory logic so that
users can select it by name in goose configuration.

Files to modify:
  1. crates/goose/src/providers/mod.rs
     → add: pub mod azure_foundry;

  2. crates/goose/src/providers/init.rs
     → add import: use super::azure_foundry::AzureFoundryProvider;
     → add in init_registry(): registry.register::<AzureFoundryProvider>(true);
       (true = shown in the UI provider picker, like OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)

Registration pattern (from init.rs study):
  registry.register::<T>(preferred: bool)
  This uses ProviderDef::metadata() for the name key ("azure_foundry") and
  ProviderDef::from_env() as the constructor. No other changes needed.

Config-file usage after registration (user-facing):
  GOOSE_PROVIDER=azure_foundry
  AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT=https://my-deployment.models.ai.azure.com
  AZURE_FOUNDRY_DEPLOYMENT=Phi-4
  AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY=<key>

Verify that the provider name "azure_foundry" does not conflict with existing
"azure_openai" (azure.rs) — they must remain separate selectable providers.
acceptance_criteria:
- Setting GOOSE_PROVIDER=azure_foundry loads AzureFoundryProvider via the registry
- goose providers list (or equivalent) shows "azure_foundry" / "Azure AI Foundry"
- Unknown provider name still returns a clear error (existing behavior, no regression)
- Existing azure_openai provider continues to work (regression test with AZURE_OPENAI_* env vars)
- cargo build succeeds with the new module wired in
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes

title: Implement AzureFoundryProvider: streaming chat completions (SSE)
description:
Extend AzureFoundryProvider to support streaming chat completions via
Server-Sent Events (SSE), following the pattern in:
  • crates/goose/src/providers/openai_compatible.rs (stream() impl)
  • crates/goose/src/providers/openai.rs (stream() dispatching)

Streaming is already handled by OpenAiCompatibleProvider if we delegate to it.
Verify that the streaming path works end-to-end for Azure Foundry endpoints.

SSE wire format (from JS SDK samples):
  • Request body: { ..., "stream": true }
  • Response: chunked transfer with SSE lines: "data: {json}\n\n"
  • Termination: "data: [DONE]\n\n"
  • Each chunk JSON has same shape as OpenAI streaming delta

Goose streaming implementation pattern (openai_compatible.rs):
  1. Call api_client.response_post() → raw reqwest::Response
  2. Wrap body: StreamReader::new(response.bytes_stream().map_err(...))
  3. FramedRead::new(reader, LinesCodec::new())
  4. async_stream::try_stream! { ... } macro iterates lines:
     - skip empty lines
     - strip "data: " prefix
     - if "[DONE]" → break
     - serde_json::from_str() → parse delta
     - call response_to_streaming_message() from goose_providers::formats::openai
     - yield (Some(message), Some(usage))
  5. Return Box::pin(stream) as MessageStream

Since AzureFoundryProvider delegates to OpenAiCompatibleProvider, confirm that
OpenAiCompatibleProvider.stream() already handles SSE correctly for the Azure Foundry
endpoint (it should, as it is fully OpenAI-compatible).

If any Azure Foundry-specific quirks are found during the spike (Task 1):
  • Note them and add adaptation logic in the stream() wrapper
  • Common issue: some endpoints require stream_options: { include_usage: true } for
    usage data in the final chunk
acceptance_criteria:
- stream() returns a MessageStream that yields tokens incrementally (not buffered)
- Verify with a mock SSE server: multi-chunk response yields N partial messages + 1 final message
- [DONE] signal terminates the stream cleanly with no error
- Mid-stream error (non-200 status or malformed JSON chunk) returns ProviderError
- Usage stats (token counts) are correctly populated from the final SSE chunk
- Unit tests with mocked SSE stream cover: full success (5 chunks), early [DONE], malformed chunk → error
- supports_streaming field is true in the constructed OpenAiCompatibleProvider

title: Implement AzureFoundryProvider: ProviderDef + Provider traits (non-streaming)
description:
Implement the Goose provider traits for Azure AI Foundry — non-streaming
chat completions first (streaming is Task 4).

File: crates/goose/src/providers/azure_foundry.rs

Implementation approach (mirror azure.rs pattern):
  • Define AzureFoundryProvider struct (unit struct — the actual runtime state
    is held by the inner OpenAiCompatibleProvider, same as AzureProvider)
  • Implement ProviderDef for AzureFoundryProvider:
      - metadata() → ProviderMetadata with name="azure_foundry", display_name="Azure AI Foundry",
        known_models (populate after spike), config_keys (AZURE_FOUNDRY_*)
      - from_env() → reads config, builds ApiClient with:
          AuthMethod::ApiKey { header_name: "api-key".to_string(), key } for API key auth
          AuthMethod::Custom(Box::new(AzureAuthProvider { auth })) for Entra ID
        Then constructs OpenAiCompatibleProvider with:
          name = AZURE_FOUNDRY_PROVIDER_NAME
          api_client = the built ApiClient (host = endpoint)
          completions_prefix = "" (MaaS path is just /chat/completions, no deployment prefix)
  • Implement Provider trait by delegating to OpenAiCompatibleProvider
    (same as AzureProvider — ProviderDef::Provider = OpenAiCompatibleProvider)

HTTP request mapping:
  POST {endpoint}/chat/completions
  Header: api-key: {api_key}   (or Authorization: Bearer {token} for Entra ID)
  Body: OpenAI-compatible JSON (reuse create_request() from goose_providers::formats::openai)

Error mapping (via http_status.rs or direct match on response status):
  401 → ProviderError::Authentication
  429 → ProviderError::RateLimitExceeded (parse Retry-After header if present)
  404 → ProviderError::EndpointNotFound
  5xx → ProviderError::ServerError
  other 4xx → ProviderError::RequestFailed

Traits and files to study:
  • crates/goose/src/providers/base.rs — Provider + ProviderDef signatures
  • crates/goose/src/providers/azure.rs — closest reference implementation
  • crates/goose/src/providers/openai_compatible.rs — inner provider logic
  • crates/goose/src/providers/api_client.rs — ApiClient, AuthMethod, AuthProvider
  • crates/goose/src/providers/azureauth.rs — AzureAuth for Entra ID
acceptance_criteria:
- ProviderDef implemented: metadata() returns correct name/display/config_keys; from_env() builds provider from AZURE_FOUNDRY_* env vars
- Provider trait implemented: stream() delegates to OpenAiCompatibleProvider (non-streaming path for now, streaming added in Task 4)
- get_name() returns "azure_foundry"
- Successful chat completion request returns correct Message + ProviderUsage
- 401 Unauthorized → ProviderError::Authentication("...")
- 429 TooManyRequests → ProviderError::RateLimitExceeded with retry_delay parsed from Retry-After
- 404 Not Found → ProviderError::EndpointNotFound("...")
- 5xx → ProviderError::ServerError("...")
- Unit tests with wiremock mock server cover: success, auth error (401), rate limit (429), server error (500)
- cargo build succeeds; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes

title: Implement AzureFoundryConfig struct and env-var wiring
description:
Create the configuration struct for the Azure AI Foundry provider, following
the pattern of existing provider configs (see crates/goose/src/providers/azure.rs for
reference: uses Config::global().get_param("KEY") and get_secret("KEY")).

File to create: crates/goose/src/providers/azure_foundry.rs (initial struct section)

Required config fields and env vars:
  • AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT      (required) — e.g. https://<name>.models.ai.azure.com
                                             or https://<project>.services.ai.azure.com
  • AZURE_FOUNDRY_DEPLOYMENT    (optional) — model/deployment name; passed in request body
                                             as the "model" field; may be omitted for
                                             single-model MaaS endpoints
  • AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY       (optional, secret) — if absent, fall back to Entra ID
  • AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_VERSION   (optional) — default: "" (not needed for MaaS endpoints)

Naming convention: follow existing azure.rs which uses AZURE_OPENAI_* prefix.
Use AZURE_FOUNDRY_* to avoid collisions.

Implementation note: reuse AzureAuth from crates/goose/src/providers/azureauth.rs
for Entra ID token acquisition (same pattern as AzureProvider in azure.rs).

Provider name constant: const AZURE_FOUNDRY_PROVIDER_NAME: &str = "azure_foundry";
acceptance_criteria:
- Config is read via Config::global().get_param / get_secret in from_env()
- Missing AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT produces a clear error message
- AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY absent causes graceful fallback to Entra ID (AzureAuth::DefaultCredential)
- AZURE_FOUNDRY_DEPLOYMENT absent is accepted (empty string / None) — used as model field in request
- Provider name constant is "azure_foundry" (snake_case, consistent with "azure_openai")
- ConfigKey entries declared in metadata() with correct required/secret flags
- Unit tests cover: all vars set with API key, missing endpoint (error), missing key (Entra fallback)

title: Spike: Azure AI Foundry API surface & auth schemes
description:
Investigate the Azure AI Foundry REST API in depth to produce a
definitive design note for the implementation tasks.

Endpoint variants to document:
  1. Serverless / MaaS:  https://<deployment>.models.ai.azure.com
     • POST /chat/completions  (no api-version, model field in body)
  2. Project-scoped Foundry:  https://<project>.services.ai.azure.com
     • Confirm exact path structure and whether api-version is required

Auth schemes:
  • API key  — header name confirmed as "api-key" (from JS SDK: apiKeyHeaderName defaults to "api-key")
  • Entra ID (DefaultAzureCredential)
    - Scope for .models.ai.azure.com: https://ml.azure.com
    - Scope for .openai.azure.com (AOAI, already covered): https://cognitiveservices.azure.com

Reference: existing azureauth.rs in crates/goose/src/providers/azureauth.rs wraps
AzureAuth which already handles DefaultAzureCredential — assess reuse.

Cross-reference: crates/goose/src/providers/azure.rs (existing azure_openai provider)
shows the pattern: AzureAuthProvider custom AuthProvider impl wrapping AzureAuth.

Deliverable: written design note captured in the Epic's design field via beads update.
acceptance_criteria:
- API key auth flow documented with exact header name ("api-key")
- Entra ID / DefaultAzureCredential flow documented with correct scope per endpoint type
- Request/response JSON schema documented (confirm OpenAI-compatible; note any differences)
- Streaming (SSE) format documented (data: {...} lines, data: [DONE] terminator)
- Error response codes mapped to ProviderError variants (401→Authentication, 429→RateLimitExceeded, 5xx→ServerError, 404→EndpointNotFound)
- Confirm whether api-version query param is needed (Foundry MaaS: NOT needed; AOAI: yes)
- Confirm whether "model" field in request body is required or optional for single-model deployments
- Design note updated in Epic issue

title: Azure AI Foundry Provider for Goose
description:
Add Azure AI Foundry as a first-class provider in the Goose Rust codebase,
alongside existing providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, etc.).

Azure AI Foundry exposes two REST endpoint styles for chat completions:
  • Serverless / MaaS (Model-as-a-Service):
      https://<deployment-name>.models.ai.azure.com
      → POST /chat/completions  (no api-version query param)
  • New AI Foundry project-scoped endpoint:
      https://<project>.services.ai.azure.com/...

Note: The existing azure.rs provider targets Azure OpenAI Service
(*.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/…) and is NOT the same service.
This epic adds a distinct provider for the AI Foundry endpoints.

Auth supports:
  • API key  →  header: api-key: <key>
  • Entra ID (DefaultAzureCredential)  →  Authorization: Bearer <token>
    Scope for .models.ai.azure.com endpoints: https://ml.azure.com

The chat completions wire format is fully OpenAI-compatible (same JSON shape),
so we can reuse the OpenAiCompatibleProvider inner adapter (like azure.rs does).
design:
## Architecture Findings (from codebase research)

### Provider Trait
File: crates/goose/src/providers/base.rs

Two traits must be implemented:
  1. ProviderDef (factory) – provides metadata() + from_env() BoxFuture
  2. Provider (runtime, async_trait) – primary method is stream():
       async fn stream(&self, model_config, session_id, system, messages, tools)
         -> Result<MessageStream, ProviderError>
     where MessageStream = Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = Result<(Option<Message>, Option<ProviderUsage>), ProviderError>> + Send>>

### HTTP Client
Crate: reqwest (workspace dep with json, stream, http2 features)
Wrapper: ApiClient struct in crates/goose/src/providers/api_client.rs
  • ApiClient::new(host: String, auth: AuthMethod) -> Result<Self>
  • api_client.response_post(session_id, path, &payload) -> Result<reqwest::Response>
  • Auth enum: AuthMethod::ApiKey { header_name: "api-key", key } for Azure API key
  • Auth enum: AuthMethod::Custom(Box<dyn AuthProvider>) for Entra ID token refresh

### Streaming
Pattern: Manual SSE parsing (same as openai_compatible.rs):
  • Call response_post() → get back a raw reqwest::Response
  • Body is byte stream; wrap with StreamReader + FramedRead + LinesCodec
  • Iterate lines; skip empty lines; parse "data: {json}" lines
  • Handle "data: [DONE]" termination sentinel
  • Yield tokens via async_stream::try_stream! macro

### Config Pattern
Config::global().get_param("KEY_NAME")  // regular config / env var
Config::global().get_secret("KEY_NAME") // sensitive / secret values

### Error Mapping
goose_providers::errors::ProviderError variants:
  • Authentication(String)               ← HTTP 401
  • RateLimitExceeded { details, retry_delay } ← HTTP 429 + Retry-After header
  • ServerError(String)                  ← HTTP 5xx
  • NetworkError(String)                 ← connection / timeout
  • EndpointNotFound(String)             ← HTTP 404
  • RequestFailed(String)                ← other 4xx

### Registration
mod.rs:  pub mod azure_foundry;
init.rs: registry.register::<AzureFoundryProvider>(true);  // true = shown in UI picker

### Existing Azure Provider (reference, NOT this epic)
File: crates/goose/src/providers/azure.rs
Provider name: "azure_openai"
Env vars: AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY
The new provider MUST use different env var names to avoid conflicts.

### Azure AI Foundry REST API (from JS SDK reference)
Endpoint: https://<name>.models.ai.azure.com  OR  https://<project>.services.ai.azure.com
Chat completions path: POST /chat/completions
Request body: OpenAI-compatible JSON
  { messages: [{role, content}], model: "<name>", stream: bool, max_tokens, temperature, tools, ... }
Streaming: SSE — "data: {...}\n\n" chunks, terminated by "data: [DONE]"
Auth header for API key: api-key: <key>
Auth header for Entra ID: Authorization: Bearer <token>
Entra ID scope (.models.ai.azure.com): https://ml.azure.com
acceptance_criteria:
- Azure AI Foundry provider is selectable by name ("azure_foundry") in goose config
- Chat completions work end-to-end with API key auth against .models.ai.azure.com endpoints
- Streaming chat completions work (tokens yielded incrementally)
- Entra ID (DefaultAzureCredential) auth flow works for managed identity scenarios
- Integration test suite passes with mocked HTTP (no real network calls in CI)
- Documentation added to documentation/docs/getting-started/providers.md and a guide file
- Existing azure_openai provider is unaffected (regression tested)

title: Document Azure AI Foundry provider setup and configuration
description:
Add user-facing documentation for the new Azure AI Foundry provider.

Docs location (follow existing pattern):
  • Add entry to: documentation/docs/getting-started/providers.md
    (look at how existing providers like Anthropic, Azure OpenAI are documented there)
  • Create a new guide: documentation/docs/guides/azure-foundry-provider.md
    (model on: documentation/docs/guides/tanzu-ai-services.md or tanzu-cli-testing-guide.md)

Content to document:

  1. Prerequisites
     - Azure subscription with AI Foundry access
     - Model deployment name (for .models.ai.azure.com) or project endpoint

  2. Environment variables (required + optional)
     - AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT  (required) — the full endpoint URL
     - AZURE_FOUNDRY_DEPLOYMENT (optional) — model/deployment name
     - AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY  (optional) — if not set, Entra ID is used
     - AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_VERSION (optional) — defaults to empty (not needed for MaaS)

  3. Example goose config snippet
     provider: azure_foundry
     model: Phi-4  # or whatever the deployment name resolves to

  4. Endpoint types explained
     - Serverless / MaaS: https://<name>.models.ai.azure.com
     - Project-scoped: https://<project>.services.ai.azure.com

  5. Authentication guide
     - API key: set AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY
     - Entra ID: ensure az login / managed identity is configured; omit API key

  6. Troubleshooting section (at least 3 common errors):
     a. 401 Unauthorized → API key missing or wrong; or Entra ID token expired
     b. 404 Not Found → endpoint URL wrong (double-check deployment name in URL)
     c. Connection refused / timeout → check AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT scheme (https not http)
acceptance_criteria:
- Documentation file exists at documentation/docs/guides/azure-foundry-provider.md
- Entry added in documentation/docs/getting-started/providers.md
- All required env vars listed with descriptions and examples
- At least one working example config snippet is shown
- Two endpoint types (MaaS vs project-scoped) are distinguished with examples
- Auth section covers both API key and Entra ID paths
- Troubleshooting section covers at least 3 common errors with solutions
- Documentation renders correctly (no broken markdown / links)
close_reason: Full guide exists at documentation/docs/guides/azure-foundry-provider.md and providers.md entry is present. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Integration tests for AzureFoundryProvider against mock HTTP server
description:
Write integration tests for the full request/response cycle of
AzureFoundryProvider using a mock HTTP server.

Testing approach (follow crates/goose/tests/providers.rs pattern):
  • Unit-level mocks: use wiremock or httpmock crate (check which is already in Cargo.toml)
  • Tests live in: crates/goose/src/providers/azure_foundry.rs #[cfg(test)] module
    OR in a new crates/goose/tests/azure_foundry_test.rs file
  • All tests must run with cargo test (no real network calls, no env var guards)

Test scenarios:
  1. Success: non-streaming chat completion → correct Message + ProviderUsage
  2. Success: streaming chat completion (5-chunk SSE) → correct incremental tokens + final usage
  3. Auth failure: mock returns 401 → ProviderError::Authentication
  4. Rate limit: mock returns 429 + Retry-After: 30 → ProviderError::RateLimitExceeded { retry_delay: Some(30s) }
  5. Server error: mock returns 500 → ProviderError::ServerError
  6. Malformed JSON in SSE chunk → ProviderError::NetworkError (stream decode error)
  7. Config error: missing AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT → from_env() returns Err before any HTTP call
  8. 404 Not Found → ProviderError::EndpointNotFound

Mock server setup reference: look at existing provider tests in crates/goose/src/providers/
that use wiremock (e.g., openai.rs or anthropic.rs #[cfg(test)] sections).
acceptance_criteria:
- All 8 test scenarios covered with assertions on error variant and key fields
- Tests run with: cargo test -p goose  (no network, no external deps)
- Tests are isolated: each test spins up its own mock server on an ephemeral port
- No shared mutable state between tests (parallel-safe)
- SSE streaming test verifies token count matches chunk count
- Retry-After test verifies retry_delay Duration is correctly parsed
- CI passes without flakiness (no sleeps, no timing dependencies)
close_reason: All 8 test scenarios implemented and passing (11 tests total). test_429 fixed from 90s to <1s via with_no_retries(). retry_delay parsed and asserted. Clippy clean.
